original_input.requirements_text: We need a simple student helpdesk system. Students should create support tickets with title, description and category. Staff should view all tickets, assign tickets, add notes and update status. Students should receive notifications when ticket status changes. Basic authentication is required.

functional_requirements.statement: Students can create support tickets with title, description, and category.
acceptance_criteria: Students can input a title, description, and category for a ticket.
Students can successfully submit the ticket.

functional_requirements.statement: Staff can view all support tickets.
acceptance_criteria: Staff can see a list of all submitted tickets.

functional_requirements.statement: Staff can assign tickets to themselves or other staff.
acceptance_criteria: Staff can assign a ticket to a designated staff member.
The assigned staff member is updated in the ticket system.

functional_requirements.statement: Staff can add notes and update ticket status.
acceptance_criteria: Staff can attach notes to a ticket.
Staff can update ticket status to Open, In Progress, or Closed.

functional_requirements.statement: Students receive notifications when ticket status changes.
acceptance_criteria: Students receive an email when the status of their ticket changes.

functional_requirements.statement: Basic authentication is required for both students and staff.
acceptance_criteria: User login requires a valid username and password.
Unauthorized users cannot access the system.

non_functional_requirements.statement: The system should have an intuitive user interface.
The system should handle up to 1000 concurrent users.

assumptions: All students and staff have internet access.
Email addresses for notifications are pre-registered in the system.
clarification_questions: What are the categories available for ticket classification?
Should notifications be sent via other methods besides email?

glossary.definition: A record of a request for assistance submitted by a student.
A method for an HTTP user agent to provide a user name and password when making a request.

files.purpose: Project overview, setup instructions, and usage guide
files.content:
# Student Helpdesk System

## Overview
This project is a helpdesk system for students to create support tickets.

## Setup Instructions
1. Clone the repository.
2. Install dependencies using `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
3. Run the application using `python app.py`.

## Usage
Access the application at `http://localhost:5000`.

files.purpose: Application entry point with Flask app initialization
files.content:
from flask import Flask
from app.routes import main_routes

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///tickets.db'

app.register_blueprint(main_routes)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

from app import db

class Ticket(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
    description = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
    category = db.Column(db.String(50), nullable=False)
    status = db.Column(db.String(20), default='Open')  # FR-04
    assigned_to = db.Column(db.String(50), nullable=True)  # FR-03

traceability.notes: Ticket creation functionality is a placeholder.
Ticket retrieval functionality is a placeholder.
Ticket assignment functionality is a placeholder.
Ticket update functionality is a placeholder.
Notification system needs to be implemented.
User authentication needs to be implemented.
Usability is addressed in the README.
Performance considerations need to be addressed.

limitations: Current implementation is a prototype and lacks full functionality.
Email notification system is not yet implemented.

validation_output.summary: The generated artifacts partially meet the functional and non-functional requirements, with several key functionalities not yet implemented.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Placeholder for ticket creation functionality
issues: The ticket creation functionality is not fully implemented; input validation and ticket creation logic are missing.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Placeholder for ticket retrieval functionality
issues: The ticket retrieval functionality is not fully implemented; logic to retrieve and return all tickets is missing.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Placeholder for ticket assignment functionality
issues: The ticket assignment functionality is not fully implemented; logic to assign tickets to staff is missing.

evidence: app/routes/main_routes.py - Placeholder for ticket update functionality
issues: The ticket update functionality is not fully implemented; logic to update ticket status and add notes is missing.
The notification system for students when ticket status changes is not implemented at all.
User authentication functionality is not implemented at all.

evidence: README.md - Usability is addressed in the README.

issues: Performance considerations for handling up to 1000 concurrent users are not addressed.

traceability_issues: FR-05 and FR-06 have no corresponding artifacts or implementation evidence.
NFR-02 has no corresponding artifacts or implementation evidence.
code_quality_issues: Missing error handling in routes for ticket operations.
No security measures implemented for user authentication.
recommendations: Implement the ticket creation, retrieval, assignment, and update functionalities with proper input validation and error handling.
Develop the notification system for students to inform them of ticket status changes.
Implement basic authentication to secure the application.
Address performance considerations to handle up to 1000 concurrent users.
validation_output.overall_assessment: The project is in an early prototype stage with several critical functionalities missing or only partially implemented, requiring significant work to meet the outlined requirements.

deployment_output.dockerfile:
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY . /app
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
EXPOSE 5000
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
issues: Input validation not implemented in routes
